title: MinerU-Diffusion: Rethinking Document OCR as Inverse Rendering via Diffusion Decoding
task: Document OCR via diffusion-based parallel decoding
key_results: 3.2x faster decoding than autoregressive baselines; improved robustness on long documents
comments: Reframing OCR as inverse rendering is conceptually appealing, though the 3.2x speedup claim should be validated against contemporary autoregressive VLM baselines.

outputs: structured text with layout, tables, formulas

hypotheses.hypothesis: Parallel diffusion decoding reduces error propagation and latency compared to left-to-right autoregressive decoding
hypotheses.result: Confirmed: achieved 3.2x speedup with improved robustness

hypotheses.hypothesis: Diffusion models have reduced dependence on linguistic priors vs. autoregressive models
hypotheses.result: Confirmed on Semantic Shuffle benchmark

title: WildWorld: A Large-Scale Dataset for Dynamic World Modeling with Actions and Explicit State toward Generative ARPG
task: Action-conditioned world modeling dataset with explicit state annotations
key_results: 108M frames, 450+ actions, synchronized skeleton/camera/depth annotations; reveals persistent challenges in long-horizon state consistency
comments: WildWorld addresses a real gap in sim-to-real world models by providing state-aware action labels, though the benchmark's ARPG origin may not fully transfer to other domains.

hypotheses.hypothesis: Explicit state annotations enable learning of structured world dynamics better than pixels alone
hypotheses.result: Extensive experiments reveal persistent challenges in state-aware video generation, suggesting hypothesis partially unconfirmed

title: PEARL: Personalized Streaming Video Understanding Model
task: Personalized streaming video understanding with real-time feedback
key_results: PEARL-Bench: 132 videos, 2,173 fine-grained annotations; PEARL baseline achieves SOTA on 8 models with consistent improvements across 3 architectures
comments: Streaming personalization is a practical problem, but the proposed training-free method's generalization to truly novel concepts needs further validation.

hypotheses.hypothesis: Training-free strategies can improve personalized video understanding across diverse VLM architectures
hypotheses.result: Confirmed: consistent improvements on 3 distinct architectures

title: DA-Flow: Degradation-Aware Optical Flow Estimation with Diffusion Models
task: Optical flow estimation under real-world corruptions
key_results: DA-Flow substantially outperforms existing methods under severe degradation across multiple benchmarks
comments: Leveraging diffusion model features for correspondence is clever, but quantitative benchmarks and comparisons are needed.

model_io.model: DA-Flow (diffusion features + CNN features + iterative refinement)

hypotheses.hypothesis: Image restoration diffusion models' intermediate representations contain corruption-aware but non-temporal information useful for optical flow
hypotheses.result: Confirmed: adding spatio-temporal attention yields zero-shot correspondence capability

title: SpecEyes: Accelerating Agentic Multimodal LLMs via Speculative Perception and Planning
task: Accelerating agentic multimodal LLMs via speculative execution
key_results: 1.1–3.35x speedup with up to +6.7% accuracy improvement; maintains throughput under concurrent workloads
comments: Speculative execution for agentic systems is practical, but the cognitive gating mechanism's generalization beyond the tested benchmarks remains unclear.

model_io.model: SpecEyes (lightweight speculative MLLM + large MLLM + heterogeneous parallel funnel)

hypotheses.hypothesis: A lightweight tool-free MLLM can predict execution trajectory and enable early termination of expensive tool chains
hypotheses.result: Confirmed: 1.1–3.35x speedup achieved

hypotheses.hypothesis: Answer separability is a reliable confidence signal for speculative termination without oracle labels
hypotheses.result: Confirmed: cognitive gating based on answer separability works effectively

title: From Static Templates to Dynamic Runtime Graphs: A Survey of Workflow Optimization for LLM Agents
task: Survey of workflow optimization for LLM agents
key_results: Unified framework organizing static vs. dynamic workflow design; structure-aware evaluation perspective proposed
comments: Comprehensive survey providing useful vocabulary and taxonomy, but lacks empirical comparisons of different optimization strategies.

title: SIMART: Decomposing Monolithic Meshes into Sim-ready Articulated Assets via MLLM
task: Part-level decomposition and kinematic prediction for articulated 3D assets
key_results: 70% token reduction via Sparse 3D VQ-VAE; SOTA on PartNet-Mobility; enables physics-based robotic simulation
comments: Using MLLMs for sim-ready asset generation is novel, but comparison with traditional multi-stage pipelines needs quantitative error analysis.

hypotheses.hypothesis: Sparse 3D tokenization enables unified joint reasoning over static and kinematic structure
hypotheses.result: Confirmed: 70% token reduction while achieving SOTA performance

title: UniGRPO: Unified Policy Optimization for Reasoning-Driven Visual Generation
task: Unified policy optimization for reasoning-driven image generation
key_results: Seamlessly integrates GRPO for reasoning and FlowGRPO for image synthesis; enhanced image quality through reasoning
comments: Unified RL framework for interleaved generation is interesting, but multi-round evaluation and comparison with baseline methods is limited.

hypotheses.hypothesis: MSE penalty on velocity fields provides more robust regularization than KL penalty for mitigating reward hacking
hypotheses.result: Confirmed: MSE penalty more effective than standard latent KL

hypotheses.hypothesis: Removing classifier-free guidance maintains linear rollouts necessary for scaling to multi-turn generation
hypotheses.result: Confirmed: linear unbranched rollouts achieved

title: RealMaster: Lifting Rendered Scenes into Photorealistic Video
task: Lifting rendered scenes to photorealistic video
key_results: Preserves 3D geometry/dynamics of rendered input while transforming materials/lighting; outperforms existing video editing baselines
comments: Anchor-based propagation strategy for paired-data generation is clever, but evaluation limited to GTA-V sequences.

hypotheses.hypothesis: Diffusion features from anchor-enhanced frames can propagate realism across intermediate frames via geometric conditioning
hypotheses.result: Confirmed: method successfully maintains 3D consistency while enhancing photorealism

title: 2Xplat: Two Experts Are Better Than One Generalist
task: Pose-free feed-forward 3D Gaussian splatting via two-expert design
key_results: Outperforms pose-free baselines; matches posed methods in <5K iterations; separates geometry from appearance estimation
comments: Two-expert design is intuitive, but the claim that unified architectures are 'suboptimal' needs more rigorous ablation across architectures.

hypotheses.hypothesis: Explicitly separating geometry and appearance improves 3DGS fidelity vs. monolithic unified models
hypotheses.result: Confirmed: two-expert design substantially outperforms unified approaches

title: Rethinking Token-Level Policy Optimization for Multimodal Chain-of-Thought
task: Token-level policy optimization for multimodal chain-of-thought reasoning
key_results: PEPO improves robustness across geometry, visual grounding, puzzles, and few-shot classification; stable training dynamics
comments: Token-level granularity for RL is appropriate for reasoning, but the perception prior derivation could be better motivated theoretically.

hypotheses.hypothesis: Token-level advantages derived from perception-exploration decomposition improve reasoning stability
hypotheses.result: Confirmed: consistent improvements with stable training across benchmarks

title: Attend Before Attention: Efficient and Scalable Video Understanding via Autoregressive Gazing
task: Efficient long-form video understanding via autoregressive patch selection
key_results: 4x–100x visual token reduction; up to 19x ViT/MLLM acceleration; enables 1K-frame 4K-resolution video processing; 67.0% on VideoMME
comments: AutoGaze's patch selection strategy is practical, and HLVid provides a needed high-resolution benchmark, though the method's applicability to very long sequences (>1K frames) needs exploration.

model_io.model: AutoGaze (lightweight selection module trained via next-token prediction + RL)

hypotheses.hypothesis: Autoregressive patch selection trained on next-token prediction can reduce visual tokens while preserving information
hypotheses.result: Confirmed: 4x–100x reduction with minimal information loss

hypotheses.hypothesis: RL fine-tuning improves selection under user-specified error thresholds
hypotheses.result: Confirmed: on-policy optimization improves patch selection quality

title: VP-VLA: Visual Prompting as an Interface for Vision-Language-Action Models
task: Spatial grounding interface for vision-language-action models
key_results: 5% improvement on Robocasa-GR1-Tabletop; 8.3% on SimplerEnv; improves over QwenOFT and GR00T baselines
comments: Decoupling reasoning and execution via visual prompts is intuitive, but evaluation is limited to simulation environments; real-world validation needed.

hypotheses.hypothesis: Explicit spatial prompting (crosshairs, bounding boxes) improves precision vs. end-to-end black-box mapping
hypotheses.result: Confirmed: 5–8.3% success rate improvement

title: ThinkJEPA: Empowering Latent World Models with Large Vision-Language Reasoning Model
task: VLM-guided latent world modeling for long-horizon forecasting
key_results: Outperforms VLM-only and JEPA-only baselines; more robust long-horizon rollout behavior
comments: Combining dense-frame dynamics with uniformly-sampled semantic guidance is well-motivated, but quantitative improvements on benchmark tasks are not clearly stated.

model_io.model: ThinkJEPA (dense JEPA branch + VLM thinker branch with hierarchical pyramid extraction)

hypotheses.hypothesis: Dual-temporal pathways combining fine-grained dynamics and long-horizon semantics improve forecast robustness
hypotheses.result: Confirmed: outperforms single-modality baselines

title: AgentSLR: Automating Systematic Literature Reviews in Epidemiology with Agentic AI
task: Automating systematic literature reviews in epidemiology
key_results: 58x speed-up (7 weeks → 20 hours); performance comparable to human researchers on 9 WHO-priority pathogens
comments: Impressive application of agentic AI to evidence synthesis, but the 'comparable to human' claim needs clearer definition of agreement metrics and inter-rater reliability baselines.

model_io.model: AgentSLR (agentic pipeline with LLM calls, retrieval, extraction, synthesis)
outputs: systematic review report with extracted data

hypotheses.hypothesis: Agentic AI can automate end-to-end systematic review workflow with performance parity to human experts
hypotheses.result: Confirmed: 58x speed-up with comparable accuracy on tested domains

title: MultiBind: A Benchmark for Attribute Misbinding in Multi-Subject Generation
task: Attribute misbinding evaluation in multi-subject image generation
key_results: MultiBind reveals binding failures missed by conventional metrics; dimension-wise confusion protocol separates self-degradation from cross-subject interference
comments: The benchmark is well-designed for a real failure mode in multi-subject generation, but only tested on static image generation; video generation evaluation would strengthen the work.

hypotheses.hypothesis: Dimension-wise confusion metrics can separate self-degradation from true cross-subject misbinding better than holistic fidelity metrics
hypotheses.result: Confirmed: MultiBind exposes interpretable failure patterns (drift, swap, dominance, blending)

title: TrajLoom: Dense Future Trajectory Generation from Video
task: Dense future trajectory generation from video
key_results: Extends prediction horizon from 24 to 81 frames; improves motion realism and stability; supports downstream video generation/editing
comments: Three-component framework is well-designed, but comparison with other trajectory prediction methods lacks detail; benefits to downstream generation tasks are claimed but not rigorously evaluated.

hypotheses.hypothesis: Offset encoding from pixel-center anchors reduces location-dependent bias in trajectory prediction
hypotheses.result: Confirmed: enables learning over longer horizons

hypotheses.hypothesis: Flow matching with boundary cues enables stable long-horizon trajectory generation
hypotheses.result: Confirmed: extends prediction from 24 to 81 frames with improved stability

title: Ego2Web: A Web Agent Benchmark Grounded in Egocentric Videos
task: Web agent benchmark grounded in egocentric video perception
key_results: First benchmark pairing egocentric video with web tasks; Ego2WebJudge achieves ~84% agreement with human judgment; SoTA agents show weak performance with substantial headroom
comments: Addresses a real gap in agent benchmarking by combining physical and digital perception, though the 84% judge agreement suggests room for improvement in automated evaluation.

hypotheses.hypothesis: Egocentric visual grounding is necessary for web agent performance on real-world scenarios
hypotheses.result: Confirmed: agents show weak performance, indicating substantial task difficulty

title: Sparse but Critical: A Token-Level Analysis of Distributional Shifts in RLVR Fine-Tuning of LLMs
task: Token-level analysis of distributional shifts in RLVR fine-tuning
key_results: Only small fraction of tokens show meaningful divergence; sparse changes directly responsible for RL gains; cross-sampling interventions isolate critical tokens
comments: Fine-grained mechanistic analysis is thorough, but findings may be specific to tested datasets/models; generalization to other RL objectives needs investigation.

model_io.model: RLVR fine-tuning (base LLM with RL policy gradient)

hypotheses.hypothesis: RLVR induces sparse, targeted token distribution shifts rather than wholesale changes
hypotheses.result: Confirmed: only small fraction of tokens meaningfully diverge

hypotheses.hypothesis: Small number of RL-sampled tokens inserted into base generations recovers RL performance gains
hypotheses.result: Confirmed: cross-sampling experiments isolate critical token decisions

title: Uncertainty-guided Compositional Alignment with Part-to-Whole Semantic Representativeness in Hyperbolic Vision-Language Models
task: Hierarchical part-to-whole alignment in hyperbolic vision-language models
key_results: SOTA on zero-shot classification, retrieval, multi-label classification; models part-to-whole semantic representativeness with hyperbolic uncertainty
comments: Uncertainty-guided compositional alignment is novel, but the practical impact of capturing hierarchical structure on downstream applications remains unclear.

hypotheses.hypothesis: Modeling part-to-whole semantic representativeness via hyperbolic uncertainty improves compositional understanding
hypotheses.result: Confirmed: SOTA on multi-object scene understanding benchmarks

title: VISion On Request: Enhanced VLLM efficiency with sparse, dynamically selected, vision-language interactions
task: Efficient LVLM inference via sparse vision-language interactions
key_results: Matches/exceeds SOTA while drastically reducing computational cost; excels in challenging tasks requiring fine-grained visual understanding
comments: VISOR's approach of sparsifying interactions rather than compressing images is practical, though the dynamic allocation policy's per-sample overhead and complexity are not fully characterized.

model_io.model: VISOR (universal network with dynamic self-attention allocation)

hypotheses.hypothesis: Sparsifying vision-language interactions reduces cost while preserving performance on fine-grained tasks
hypotheses.result: Confirmed: matches SOTA with reduced computational cost

hypotheses.hypothesis: Dynamic policy allocation based on per-sample complexity improves efficiency
hypotheses.result: Confirmed: adaptive allocation improves results on challenging tasks

title: ABot-PhysWorld: Interactive World Foundation Model for Robotic Manipulation with Physics Alignment
task: Physics-aware video world model for robotic manipulation
key_results: SOTA on PBench and EZSbench vs. Veo 3.1, Sora v2 Pro; suppresses unphysical behaviors while preserving visual quality; enables physics-based simulation
comments: DPO-based post-training for physics alignment is practical, but the EZSbench benchmark's ability to generalize to diverse manipulation tasks beyond the training distribution is unclear.

model_io.model: ABot-PhysWorld (14B Diffusion Transformer with DPO post-training)

hypotheses.hypothesis: DPO with decoupled discriminators suppresses unphysical behaviors better than standard likelihood objectives
hypotheses.result: Confirmed: SOTA physical plausibility on PBench and EZSbench

title: VTAM: Video-Tactile-Action Models for Complex Physical Interaction Beyond VLAs
task: Multimodal world modeling incorporating tactile perception
key_results: 90% average success rate on contact-rich manipulation; 80% improvement over pi 0.5 baseline on force-aware tasks like chip pick-and-place
comments: Integrating tactile modality is well-motivated for contact-rich tasks, but the 80% improvement claim against pi 0.5 needs context (is pi 0.5 vision-only or multimodal?).

hypotheses.hypothesis: Tactile perception regularization prevents visual latent dominance in multimodal fusion
hypotheses.result: Confirmed: balanced cross-modal attention achieved

hypotheses.hypothesis: Tactile feedback corrects visual estimation errors in contact-critical scenarios
hypotheses.result: Confirmed: 90% success on contact-rich tasks vs. lower vision-only baselines

title: Regulating AI Agents
task: Regulatory framework analysis for AI agents under EU AI Act
key_results: Identifies mismatch between Act's design (for conventional AI) and challenges of AI agents; proposes institutional and enforcement improvements
comments: Policy analysis is thorough but primarily descriptive; concrete proposals for regulatory amendments would strengthen the work.

title: Reconstruction-Guided Slot Curriculum: Addressing Object Over-Fragmentation in Video Object-Centric Learning
task: Object-centric video decomposition with reconstruction-guided slot curriculum
key_results: FG-ARI gains of +6.8 on YouTube-VIS, +8.3 on MOVi-C; addresses over-fragmentation via progressive slot allocation
comments: Slot curriculum and structure-aware loss address real limitations, but the cyclic inference mechanism's contribution to temporal consistency could be isolated more clearly.

hypotheses.hypothesis: Progressive slot allocation prevents over-fragmentation by expanding capacity where reconstruction fails
hypotheses.result: Confirmed: FG-ARI improvements of +6.8 to +8.3

hypotheses.hypothesis: Structure-aware loss sharpens semantic boundaries better than MSE alone
hypotheses.result: Confirmed: local contrast preservation improves slot semantics

title: Reasoning or Rhetoric? An Empirical Analysis of Moral Reasoning Explanations in Large Language Models
task: Empirical analysis of moral reasoning in large language models
key_results: Models exhibit post-conventional reasoning regardless of size/architecture (inverse of human norms); widespread moral decoupling (inconsistency between stated reasoning and action); near-robotic cross-dilemma consistency
comments: Thorough empirical study reveals concerning patterns, but the 'moral ventriloquism' hypothesis conflates rhetorical surface-level sophistication with developmental trajectory in ways that may oversimplify LLM cognition.

model_io.model: LLM-as-judge (multiple judge models for moral stage classification)

hypotheses.hypothesis: Alignment training produces post-conventional reasoning responses across all models regardless of scale
hypotheses.result: Confirmed: striking inversion of human developmental norms

hypotheses.hypothesis: Models exhibit systematic inconsistency between stated moral justification and action choice
hypotheses.result: Confirmed: moral decoupling persists across scale and prompting

title: STEM Agent: A Self-Adapting, Tool-Enabled, Extensible Architecture for Multi-Protocol AI Agent Systems
task: Modular architecture for multi-protocol AI agent systems
key_results: Unifies 5 interoperability protocols; Caller Profiler learns 20+ behavioral dimensions; skills acquisition system achieves sub-linear memory growth; 413-test suite validates all layers in <3 seconds
comments: STEM Agent's modularity and extensibility are well-designed, but lack of comparison with other agent frameworks limits assessment of practical advantages.

model_io.model: STEM Agent (modular core with protocol handlers, tool bindings, memory subsystems)

hypotheses.hypothesis: Biological pluripotency-inspired differentiation enables a single agent core to adapt across diverse protocols
hypotheses.result: Confirmed: unified gateway unifies 5 protocols

hypotheses.hypothesis: Episodic pruning and semantic deduplication prevent super-linear memory growth
hypotheses.result: Confirmed: sub-linear growth under sustained interaction

title: Session Risk Memory (SRM): Temporal Authorization for Deterministic Pre-Execution Safety Gates
task: Temporal authorization for distributed attack detection in AI agents
key_results: SRM achieves F1=1.0000 (0% false positive) on slow-burn attacks; per-turn overhead <250 microseconds; extends stateless gate coverage to trajectory level
comments: Session Risk Memory's deterministic approach to temporal authorization is elegant and efficient, though evaluation is limited to 80 synthetic sessions; real-world attack patterns may differ.

model_io.model: SRM (semantic centroid tracking with exponential moving average risk signal)

hypotheses.hypothesis: Session-level risk tracking detects distributed attacks missed by stateless per-action gates
hypotheses.result: Confirmed: F1=1.0000 on multi-turn attack scenarios

hypotheses.hypothesis: Compact semantic centroid representation enables sub-millisecond per-turn authorization
hypotheses.result: Confirmed: <250 microsecond overhead

title: Biomolecular condensates mediate C–N bond formation
task: Determine if intrinsically disordered protein condensates catalyze nonenzymatic C–N bond formation
key_results: Condensates drive reductive amination of amines with aldehydes/ketones to form imines and alkylated amines; metabolomics identified previously unknown metabolites from amine-ketone/aldehyde dimerization in cells
comments: A striking example of how phase-separated biomolecular condensates exhibit emergent chemical reactivity without intrinsic enzymatic domains, suggesting condensates regulate metabolism beyond protein sequestration.

hypotheses.hypothesis: Condensates without inherent enzymatic activity can spontaneously facilitate reductive amination
hypotheses.result: Confirmed: condensates promote C–N bond formation between amines and carbonyl compounds

hypotheses.hypothesis: Condensate-mediated reactions generate metabolites not produced in dispersed conditions
hypotheses.result: Confirmed: combinatorial metabolomics revealed previously unknown amine-ketone/aldehyde dimerization products

title: E3 ubiquitin ligase Stub1 enhances viral replication by promoting TBK1 degradation through molecular chaperone-mediated autophagy
task: Characterize role of E3 ligase Stub1 in TBK1 degradation via chaperone-mediated autophagy during viral infection
key_results: Stub1 targets TBK1 for CMA-mediated degradation through K344 ubiquitination (K27-linked); HSC70/HspA8 recognizes ubiquitinated TBK1 for LAMP2A-mediated autophagic degradation; Stub1-deficient mice show increased VSV/HSV-1 resistance and elevated type I IFN
comments: Identifies TBK1-Stub1 axis as CMA-dependent inhibitor of antiviral immunity; mechanistic detail on ubiquitin linkage specificity is noteworthy; potential therapeutic target for enhancing antiviral responses.

hypotheses.hypothesis: Stub1 inhibits antiviral immunity by targeting TBK1 for CMA
hypotheses.result: Confirmed; Stub1 deficiency increases antiviral resistance

hypotheses.hypothesis: K27-linked ubiquitination of TBK1 at K344 targets it to LAMP2A-mediated autophagy
hypotheses.result: Confirmed; U73122 blocks the TBK1-Stub1 interaction and ubiquitination

title: Transcription-dependent and -independent functions of Drosophila p53 isoforms in the induction of apoptosis and senescence-associated tumorigenesis
task: Dissect isoform-specific mechanisms of p53-mediated apoptosis and senescence-associated tumorigenesis
key_results: p53-B triggers transcription-independent apoptosis via Dronc activation; all isoforms promote tumorigenesis through JNK-mediated senescence; findings partly conserved in human p53
comments: Elegant use of Drosophila genetics to reveal complexity of p53 isoform function; transcription-independent apoptotic mechanism is noteworthy but applicability to mammals requires validation.

hypotheses.hypothesis: p53-B induces apoptosis independently of cell cycle status
hypotheses.result: Confirmed; p53-B but not p53-A/E activates Dronc in non-proliferative cells

hypotheses.hypothesis: p53 isoforms promote tumorigenesis via JNK-mediated senescence
hypotheses.result: Confirmed; senescent cells accumulate when apoptosis is blocked

title: Malic enzyme 1 contributes to tumorigenesis and lenvatinib resistance in hepatocellular carcinoma via FSP1-dependent ferroptosis evasion
task: Define ME1 role in HCC tumorigenesis and lenvatinib resistance via ferroptosis suppression
key_results: ME1 upregulated in HCC and correlates with poor prognosis; ME1-dependent NADPH production activates FSP1-CoQH2 axis to block ferroptosis; Me1 knockout restores lenvatinib sensitivity
comments: Well-mechanized study linking metabolism to drug resistance; ferroptosis evasion as resistance mechanism is increasingly relevant but the NADPH-FSP1-CoQH2 axis may be druggable.

hypotheses.hypothesis: ME1 promotes HCC progression through ferroptosis inhibition
hypotheses.result: Confirmed; ME1 overexpression confers lenvatinib resistance via NADPH-FSP1-CoQH2 axis

hypotheses.hypothesis: ME1 knockout restores drug sensitivity
hypotheses.result: Confirmed; Me1 deletion reduces tumor burden and re-sensitizes cells to lenvatinib

title: Lactate-mediated macrophage polarization promotes splenomegaly in acute erythroleukemia
task: Elucidate lactate-mediated macrophage polarization driving splenomegaly in acute erythroleukemia
key_results: Lactate directly induces M1 macrophage polarization; self-reinforcing cycle generates lactate accumulation in splenic microenvironment; Oxamate (lactate inhibitor) reverses M1 polarization, ameliorates splenomegaly and extends survival
comments: Identifies lactate as a key immunomodulatory factor in AEL pathogenesis; the feedback amplification loop is elegant but specificity of lactate effects versus other glycolytic metabolites warrants clarification.

hypotheses.hypothesis: Lactate drives M1 macrophage polarization in AEL splenic microenvironment
hypotheses.result: Confirmed; lactate alone induces M1 polarization in vitro

hypotheses.hypothesis: Lactate-driven M1 polarization creates self-reinforcing cycle
hypotheses.result: Confirmed; M1 macrophages increase glycolytic activity and lactate production

title: TPTE, a testis-specific PTEN family member, drives spermatogenesis via PI(4,5)P2 synthesis
task: Establish role of TPTE phosphatase in spermatogenesis via PI(4,5)P2 synthesis
key_results: TPTE-deficiency reduced sperm count and motility; TPTE regulates AKT/mTOR-dependent PDLIM1 translation during spermiogenesis and RAD50 expression during meiosis
comments: Novel mechanistic link between a testis-specific PTEN family member and male infertility through lipid signaling; potential clinical relevance to non-obstructive azoospermia.

hypotheses.hypothesis: TPTE generates PI(4,5)P2 to inhibit AKT/mTOR signaling during spermiogenesis
hypotheses.result: Confirmed; TPTE deficiency impairs flagellar development through dysregulation of PDLIM1 translation

hypotheses.hypothesis: TPTE regulates DNA double-strand break repair during meiosis
hypotheses.result: Confirmed; TPTE controls RAD50 expression and homologous recombination initiation

title: SLIT3 fragments orchestrate neurovascular expansion and thermogenesis in brown adipose tissue
task: Characterize SLIT3 fragment-mediated coordination of brown adipogenesis, angiogenesis, and sympathetic innervation
key_results: SLIT3 cleaved into SLIT3-N (angiogenesis) and SLIT3-C (sympathetic innervation) fragments; PLXNA1 identified as SLIT3-C receptor essential for sympathetic innervation; BMP1 identified as first vertebrate SLIT protease; coordinated neurovascular response to cold exposure
comments: Elegant bifurcated signaling mechanism ensuring synchronized brown adipose tissue activation; identification of BMP1 as SLIT protease is novel; adipocyte progenitors emerge as key regulators of tissue innervation.

hypotheses.hypothesis: SLIT3 fragments mediate tissue crosstalk during brown adipogenesis
hypotheses.result: Confirmed; distinct SLIT3-N and SLIT3-C fragments coordinate angiogenesis and innervation

hypotheses.hypothesis: PLXNA1 is essential for SLIT3-C-mediated sympathetic innervation
hypotheses.result: Confirmed; PLXNA1 identified as SLIT3-C receptor required for innervation

hypotheses.hypothesis: BMP1 cleaves SLIT3 in vertebrates
hypotheses.result: Confirmed; BMP1 identified as first described vertebrate SLIT protease

title: Therapeutic potential of dihydronicotinamide riboside (NRH) on obesity and glucose intolerance in mice
task: Evaluate therapeutic potential of NAD+ precursor NRH on metabolic disease
key_results: Chronic NRH (100 mg/kg/day) well-tolerated with minimal effects on normal diet; mitigates high-fat diet-induced glucose intolerance and improves hepatic lipid catabolism gene expression
comments: Promising preventive and therapeutic agent for metabolic complications, but dose-dependent toxicity and limited efficacy in normal conditions suggest need for optimization in target populations.

hypotheses.hypothesis: NRH improves metabolic outcomes in high-fat diet-induced obesity
hypotheses.result: Confirmed; improved glucose tolerance and increased lipid catabolism genes

title: Brain-derived microvesicles induce activation of aspirin-treated platelets via the PLC/PKC pathway
task: Elucidate molecular mechanisms of BDMV-mediated platelet activation in aspirin-treated cells
key_results: BDMVs express COX-1 and activate aspirin-treated platelets through PLC/PKC/Akt phosphorylation; U73122 (PLC inhibitor) blocks PKC phosphorylation; BDMV+arachidonic acid synergistically activate platelets
comments: Identifies compensatory mechanism for platelet activation post-hemorrhage despite aspirin therapy; COX-1 presence on BDMVs may partially explain aspirin-resistant thrombosis.

hypotheses.hypothesis: BDMVs activate aspirin-treated platelets via PLC/PKC pathway
hypotheses.result: Confirmed; U73122 blockade of PLC reduces downstream PKC phosphorylation

hypotheses.hypothesis: COX-1 on BDMVs counteracts aspirin inhibition
hypotheses.result: Confirmed; BDMVs express COX-1 and synergize with arachidonic acid to activate platelets

title: Heat shock factor-1 alleviates ER-stress in Caenorhabditis elegans
task: Determine role of heat shock factor-1 in endoplasmic reticulum unfolded protein response
key_results: HSF-1 is required for efficient UPRER activation in C. elegans; HSF-1 activity essential for tunicamycin-induced ER stress tolerance; mRNA levels of UPRER genes regulated by HSF1 in human cell lines
comments: Reveals cross-talk between heat shock response and ER stress response pathways; HSF-1 regulation of UPRER genes suggests broader role in cellular stress coordination than previously appreciated.

model_io.model: HSF-1 in C. elegans stress response

hypotheses.hypothesis: HSF-1 is required for efficient UPRER activation
hypotheses.result: Confirmed; HSF-1 deficiency impairs ER stress response gene induction

hypotheses.hypothesis: HSF-1 regulates UPRER genes across species
hypotheses.result: Confirmed; HSF1-regulated UPRER mRNA levels detected in human cell lines

title: EphA2 monoclonal antibody attenuates hyperoxia-induced acute lung injury by preserving the alveolar–endothelial barrier
task: Determine role of EphA2/ephrinA1 signaling in hyperoxia-induced acute lung injury and barrier preservation
key_results: Hyperoxia increases phospho-EphA2 and disrupts VE-cadherin, ZO-2, claudin-5; anti-EphA2 mAb pretreatment preserved junctional proteins, enhanced Akt phosphorylation, reduced apoptosis and oxidative stress, improved survival (p=0.045)
comments: Demonstrates pharmacological targeting of EphA2 as barrier-protective strategy under hyperoxic stress; mechanistic link between Eph signaling and tight junction maintenance is well-supported.

hypotheses.hypothesis: EphA2 activation disrupts alveolar-endothelial barrier during hyperoxia
hypotheses.result: Confirmed; hyperoxia increases phospho-EphA2 and disrupts junctional proteins

hypotheses.hypothesis: EphA2 blockade preserves barrier integrity and improves outcomes
hypotheses.result: Confirmed; anti-EphA2 mAb preserves junctional proteins and extends survival

title: Rejuvenating the damaged and aged heart: lessons from development
task: Propose developmental reprogramming strategy to restore cardiac regenerative capacity in adult hearts
key_results: Cardiac regeneration traded for high-output contractility during evolution; developmental trade-off involves metabolic/epigenetic regulation and microenvironmental maturation; cardiac aging recapitulates disruption of developmental regulatory mechanisms
comments: Conceptual review proposing reactivation of juvenile regulatory networks through transcription factor induction, metabolic reprogramming, and niche modulation; lacks experimental validation but provides strategic framework for future studies.

hypotheses.hypothesis: Reactivating juvenile regulatory networks can restore cardiomyocyte proliferative potential
hypotheses.result: Proposed but not yet experimentally validated

hypotheses.hypothesis: Cardiac aging mechanisms mirror developmental withdrawal from cell cycle
hypotheses.result: Proposed based on comparative regulatory analysis

title: A bio-inspired origami capacitive robotic e-skin with multimodal sensing capabilities
task: Super-resolution tactile sensing via origami-inspired capacitive e-skin with ML enhancement
key_results: 60000 mm² coverage; super-resolution factor 241; localization error <3.5 mm, force magnitude error <0.04 N; multi-point touch for non-adjacent loads via integrated theoretical models and ML
comments: Bio-inspired origami design with shear and proximity sensing; ML-enhanced super-resolution achieves practical tactile feedback for human-machine interaction.

inputs: capacitive sensor arrays (interdigital electrodes, proximity layer)
outputs: touch location, force magnitude, proximity detection

hypotheses.hypothesis: Origami deformation transmission enables super-resolution tactile sensing via ML
hypotheses.result: 241× super-resolution achieved with <3.5mm localization and <0.04N force error

title: An aerial color image anomaly dataset for search missions in complex forested terrain
task: Benchmark dataset creation for aerial anomaly detection in forests
key_results: Crowd-sourced labeled dataset of hard-to-detect anomalies under occlusion; existing methods performed poorly, indicating need for context-aware approaches
comments: Real-world dataset from actual search operation; limited quantitative evaluation of detection methods reported.

title: TTEA: designing a quantum-ready and energy-conscious encryption model for secure IoT environments
task: Lightweight quantum-ready encryption for resource-constrained IoT devices
key_results: TTEA reduces energy by 39%, memory by 40%, execution time by 20% vs. TEA; avalanche effect 48.5%; ciphertext entropy 7.98 bits/128B; resistant to differential/linear/side-channel attacks and quantum threats (CRYSTALS-Kyber integrated)
comments: Practical cryptographic design balancing post-quantum security with IoT energy constraints; strong security analysis across classical and quantum threats.

model_io.model: TTEA (20×20 S-box + adaptive key scheduling + CRYSTALS-Kyber)

hypotheses.hypothesis: Adaptive key scheduling reduces energy overhead while maintaining security against differential/quantum attacks
hypotheses.result: TTEA 39% energy reduction vs. TEA with avalanche 48.5% and quantum resistance via post-quantum key exchange

title: Effect of initial stress on electro-magneto-thermoelastic semiconductor materials exposed to a pulsed lasers due to the interaction between electrons and holes
task: Thermoelastic wave propagation in semiconductor materials under pulsed laser and electromagnetic coupling
key_results: Coupled model integrates photothermal, mechanical, and carrier-density effects; normal mode analysis yields analytical solutions; silicon case study shows effects of hall current, laser pulse, initial stress via MATHEMATICA simulations
comments: Novel formulation combining photo-elastic, photo-electronic deformations, and hall current effects; theoretical contribution without experimental validation.

model_io.model: Coupled thermoelastic-electromagnetic differential equations (normal mode analysis)
inputs: initial stress, laser pulse parameters, electromagnetic field
outputs: temperature, carrier density, displacement, normal stress

hypotheses.hypothesis: Initial stress significantly alters thermoelastic wave propagation in semiconductors under laser irradiation
hypotheses.result: Graphical comparison shows initial stress, electromagnetic field, and thermal effects on physical quantities

title: Research on a detection method for blast rock fragments based on concavity-based pre-segmentation
task: Point cloud segmentation of blast rock fragments via concavity-based pre-segmentation
key_results: ~80% accuracy in distinguishing rock fragment segmentation states; robust large-fragment segmentation without over/under-segmentation; superior to four state-of-the-art methods
comments: Concavity criterion, boundary estimation, and coplanarity refinement address practical mucking efficiency challenge; engineering-focused with practical validation.

inputs: LiDAR point cloud of blast rock fragment pile

hypotheses.hypothesis: Concavity-based pre-segmentation reduces over/under-segmentation in complex rock piles
hypotheses.result: Method achieved ~80% accuracy with robust large-fragment detection vs. four baseline algorithms

title: An explainable AI-driven hybrid feature selection approach for coronary artery disease diagnosis
task: Explainable hybrid feature selection for coronary artery disease diagnosis
key_results: SHOW algorithm selects 14 features (55 available) from Z-Alizadeh Sani, achieving 93.79% accuracy; 5 features (13 available) from Cleveland, 86.52% accuracy; outperforms 14 state-of-the-art methods
comments: SHAP-based ranking with sequential wrapper selection; strong clinical metrics (sensitivity, specificity, AUC); extensive validation across three datasets and seven classifiers.

hypotheses.hypothesis: SHAP-ranked feature ordering improves wrapper selection efficiency for CAD diagnosis
hypotheses.result: SHOW outperformed 14 competitive algorithms in accuracy and clinical metrics across three datasets

title: Lightweight multiscale behavior recognition for caged laying hens using an enhanced YOLOv8 framework
task: Multi-scale behavior recognition in caged laying hens via enhanced YOLOv8
key_results: mAP@50: eating 98.15%, open-mouth breathing 81.03%, self-pecking 93.65%, mutual pecking 94.32%; 2.26% overall mAP improvement with 22.92% model size reduction vs. baseline
comments: C2f-FasterNet-EMA backbone and EMASlideLoss effectively address dense housing and occlusion; strong engineering contribution to animal monitoring.

hypotheses.hypothesis: Multiscale feature extraction and sample imbalance handling improve small-action recognition
hypotheses.result: Combined C2f-FasterNet-EMA, Dysample neck, and EMASlideLoss achieved 2.26% mAP gain with 22.92% size reduction

title: Combining graphlets and random walks for capturing complex network topology
task: Network topology characterization via graphlet orbit adjacency vs. random walks
key_results: Orbit adjacency consistently outperforms random-walk embeddings on node-label prediction across 40 real-world networks; random walks capture only subset of local patterns and obscure structurally informative signals
comments: Theoretical analysis demonstrates fundamental limitations of random-walk methods; empirical validation across social, technological, biological domains.

hypotheses.hypothesis: Random walks miss higher-order structural patterns critical for topology-function relationships
hypotheses.result: Orbit adjacency representations consistently outperformed random-walk methods on node-label prediction

title: Hyper-dimensional computing for enhanced label-free particle analysis in a flow-based optical detection system
task: Label-free microparticle classification via hyper-dimensional computing and event-based imaging
key_results: 98.67% classification accuracy for four particle sizes using ground-glass diffuser; HDC achieves efficient post-processing with low computational overhead; label-free alternative to fluorescent labeling
comments: Brain-inspired HDC approach on event-driven optical patterns; proof-of-concept with synthetic polystyrene beads; scalability to biological systems remains open.

inputs: optical interference patterns from microfluidic channel (polystyrene beads 4 sizes)

hypotheses.hypothesis: Ground-glass diffuser enhances optical diversity for improved HDC-based particle classification
hypotheses.result: Classification accuracy reached 98.67% under optimal diffuser configuration

title: Automated detection of physical contact events in youth ice hockey: a player-centric deep learning approach
task: Contact event detection in youth ice hockey from single-view video
key_results: 86.4% head-impact recall; reduces manual review from 3+ hours to <30 minutes per game; TSM classifier with 1.5× crop and 1:9 imbalance ratio optimal
comments: Practical pipeline with player-centric clips; full-game evaluation shows substantial above-baseline performance under realistic class imbalance.

hypotheses.hypothesis: Player-centric temporal modeling captures contact events better than whole-frame approaches
hypotheses.result: 1s player-centric clips with TSM achieved strong detection and practical head-impact pre-filtering

title: Bidirectional state space modeling for lightweight and robust wheat head detection in complex agricultural environments
task: Wheat head detection in aerial imagery via bidirectional state space modeling
key_results: Mamba-WheatNet achieves mAP@50 superior to YOLOv13; outperforms RT-DETR with lower computational cost; state-of-the-art on VisDrone2019 benchmark
comments: Strong ablation studies; design combines selective SSM with lightweight convolutions effectively for agricultural UAV applications.

hypotheses.hypothesis: Bidirectional spatial attention reduces background interference in wheat detection
hypotheses.result: BVSSMBlock fusion improved mAP@50 over baseline YOLOv8n

title: Intelligent power underwater wireless sensor networks for marine environmental monitoring using a hybrid marine predator–Henry gas solubility optimization approach
task: Energy-efficient clustering and routing for underwater wireless sensor networks
key_results: MPA-HGSO achieves FND 2151 rounds, HND 2160 rounds vs. LEACH 1115/1290; average transmission delay 140 ms (44% reduction vs. conventional); balances energy consumption, network lifetime, and communication efficiency
comments: Hybrid metaheuristic (MPA for clustering, HGSO for routing) on 300-node network; simulation-only evaluation; strong practical relevance for marine monitoring deployments.

outputs: cluster heads, multi-hop routing paths, network lifetime

hypotheses.hypothesis: Hybrid MPA-HGSO optimization extends network lifetime vs. LEACH, TEEN, MPSO, IPSO-GWO
hypotheses.result: MPA-HGSO FND 2151 rounds, ~2× LEACH; 44% delay reduction; significantly better energy-lifetime balance

title: Refining the processing dynamics of English compound words in L2 learners: a psycholinguistic modeling approach
task: Feature importance ranking for L2 compound word processing stages via supervised ML
key_results: Frequency dominant across all processing stages; semantic features increase contribution in late stages; morphological features weak across stages
comments: Eye-tracking data with multiple ML models; findings align with opportunity-maximization theory and support psycholinguistic data-driven perspectives.

model_io.model: Decision tree, random forest, neural network, SVR

hypotheses.hypothesis: Linguistic features have differential effects across temporal processing stages
hypotheses.result: Frequency dominant early/middle; semantic features increasingly important late; morphology weak throughout

title: Beyond localized methane plume detection: a dual-path deep learning framework for sensor-agnostic global hyperspectral methane plume monitoring
task: Dual-path deep learning for sensor-agnostic hyperspectral methane plume detection
key_results: Enhancement-based models exhibit higher segmentation accuracy across plume scales; radiance-based models avoid preprocessing overhead (matched filtering) and enable rapid screening; both maintain competitive performance with explainable features consistent with CH4 absorption
comments: Expert-validated datasets from EMIT and Tanager-1; cross-sensor evaluation demonstrates architectural robustness; physics-grounded XAI validation strengthens scientific credibility.

hypotheses.hypothesis: Enhancement-based and radiance-based approaches capture complementary methane signatures
hypotheses.result: ENH higher accuracy, RAD faster with less preprocessing; both learn spatial-spectral CH4 absorption features

title: Machine learning global atomic representations with Euclidean fast attention
task: Long-range interaction modeling in molecular systems via efficient attention
key_results: Euclidean Fast Attention (EFA) achieves linear-complexity attention for molecular data; enables MLFFs to capture long-range effects conventional methods miss
comments: Novel rotary positional encoding preserves physical symmetries; evaluation limited to chemical accuracy benchmarks without explicit runtime comparisons.

hypotheses.hypothesis: EFA captures long-range correlations essential for chemical interactions
hypotheses.result: EFA-MLFFs describe challenging interactions where conventional MLFFs fail

title: A causal discovery and inference framework for on-demand food delivery delays
task: Causal discovery of on-demand food delivery delays via Bayesian causal inference and double ML
key_results: 16.7% delay rate in 405,180 Chinese OFD orders; pickup/transport duration strongest causal effects; delay propagation first identified—prior delays increase subsequent delays; actionable insights for operational optimization
comments: Novel application of Bayesian causal discovery to distinguish causal drivers from correlations in OFD; practical platform implications for reducing delays.

model_io.model: Bayesian causal discovery + double machine learning
inputs: OFD order records (order timing, courier actions, distance, weather)
outputs: causal DAG, treatment effects on delay

hypotheses.hypothesis: Pickup/transport duration and delay propagation are true causal drivers of OFD delays
hypotheses.result: Causal discovery identified pickup/transport as strongest effects; delay propagation discovered as mechanism for subsequent order delays

title: A compact digital compute-in-memory Ising annealer with probabilistic SRAM bit in 28 nm for travelling salesman problem
task: Digital compute-in-memory Ising annealer for combinatorial optimization via probabilistic SRAM
key_results: Solves 96-city TSP in 620 µs with 961 nJ energy; 15×–572× improvement in hardware cost ratio vs. prior TSP solvers; intrinsic SRAM process variations generate probabilistic bits without external RNG
comments: Hierarchical clustering and compact weight mapping reduce hardware cost; probabilistic computing via pseudo-read operations is elegant hardware-algorithm co-design.

hypotheses.hypothesis: Intrinsic SRAM process variations enable area-efficient probabilistic annealing without dedicated RNG
hypotheses.result: 96-city TSP solved in 620µs/961nJ with 15×–572× hardware cost improvement over prior solvers

title: Machine learning discovers new champion codes
task: Discovery of champion error-correcting linear codes via transformer and genetic algorithm
key_results: Transformer predicts minimum Hamming distance; combined with genetic algorithm reduces search space for champion codes; method applicable to toric, Reed–Muller, BCH, algebrogeometric, and potentially quantum codes
comments: Novel application of deep learning to classical coding theory; expands known champion codes across multiple code families.

outputs: minimum Hamming distance prediction, champion code candidate

hypotheses.hypothesis: Transformer can learn patterns in code parameters to predict minimum Hamming distance, enabling efficient champion code search
hypotheses.result: Transformer-GA method discovered new champion codes; effective for multiple code families

title: A corn shaped ultrasonic meta-buffer rod design for online temperature monitoring in additive manufacturing
task: Online high-temperature monitoring in FDM additive manufacturing via metamaterial-based buffer rod
key_results: Corn-shaped meta-buffer rod accurately records surface temperatures up to 190 °C; 60.9% mass reduction vs. conventional designs; ANN-optimized TPMS thermal channel; diffuse-wave ultrasonic channel with 7% porosity
comments: Machine-learning-assisted design of metamaterial thermal/ultrasonic channels; addresses practical FDM quality control challenge with innovative materials approach.

hypotheses.hypothesis: ANN-optimized TPMS thermal channel with ultrasonic diffuse-wave detection enables high-temperature monitoring
hypotheses.result: Meta-buffer rod records temperatures to 190°C with 60.9% mass reduction and improved print quality assessment

title: Intrinsically stretchable organic light-emitting-diode with high brightness and stretchability via elastic-microphase-engineered emitter and dual-embedded electrode
task: Developing intrinsically stretchable organic light-emitting diodes with high brightness and mechanical durability
key_results: Record luminance 33,443 cd m−2 with 120% stretchability; ~90% luminance retention after 100 cycles at 15% strain; elastic-microphase engineering in emissive layer; dual-embedded hybrid electrode
comments: Impressive optoelectronic and mechanical performance achieved through systematic materials engineering; long-term color stability and degradation mechanisms under repeated stretching remain underexplored.

hypotheses.hypothesis: Elastomer miscibility with emissive polymer regulates microphase separation and mechanical–optoelectronic properties simultaneously
hypotheses.result: Confirmed; systematic variation of block copolymer elastomers shows critical miscibility dependence

hypotheses.hypothesis: Dual-embedded PH1000@AgNWs@TPU electrode improves conductivity and stretchability
hypotheses.result: Confirmed; achieves superior electrical, mechanical, and thermal stability vs. conventional STEs

title: Extreme sensitivity label-free biosensing platform based on topologically disruptive phase nano-optics
task: Extreme-sensitivity label-free biosensing via topological phase singularity in plasmonic nanostructures
key_results: Refractive-index sensitivity 3.27×10⁸ nm RIU⁻¹; refractive-index resolution 3.67×10⁻⁷ RIU; detection of 244 Da biotin at femtomolar concentration; TNF-α detection at 100 zeptomolar (10⁻²¹ mol L⁻¹)
comments: Extraordinary sensitivity via topological phase disruption is promising; however, claims of zeptomolar detection require independent validation and comparison with state-of-the-art biosensors.

model_io.model: Silver nanoparticle array in alumina with gold overlay

hypotheses.hypothesis: Sub-nanometer silver nanoparticle spacing generates sharp topological phase singularities for label-free biosensing
hypotheses.result: Confirmed; position shift of 554.3 µm achieved with extreme refractive-index sensitivity

hypotheses.hypothesis: Platform enables detection of low-molecular-weight biomarkers at subfemtomolar concentrations
hypotheses.result: Demonstrated for 244 Da biotin and TNF-α; absolute validation methodology not detailed

title: Tunable flexible capacitive sensor for dynamic pressure monitoring
task: Engineering tunable flexible capacitive pressure sensor via buckling-guided assembly for dynamic pressure monitoring
key_results: Pressure sensitivity tunable via nonlinear electrode-spacing variation; >6000 cycle durability; ~2 Pa detection limit; 4% hysteresis; 131/140 ms response/recovery times; reversible tuning by lateral strain
comments: Elegant geometric design exploiting buckling mechanics achieves impressive performance metrics; wind-tunnel validation demonstrates practical robustness, though long-term aging in harsh environments warrants further study.

model_io.model: 3D cage-like elastomer structure with embedded electrodes

hypotheses.hypothesis: Nonlinear electrode-spacing variation enables load-dependent sensitivity tuning
hypotheses.result: Confirmed; low sensitivity at low loads, enhanced sensitivity at higher loads

hypotheses.hypothesis: Reversible tuning via lateral strain and liquid encapsulation improves environmental robustness
hypotheses.result: Confirmed through mechanical testing and wind-tunnel experiments

title: Compact and low mutual coupling 4 × 4 wideband MIMO antenna design for 5G millimeter-wave applications
task: Design of compact wideband 4×4 MIMO antenna array for 5G millimeter-wave FR2 applications
key_results: Impedance bandwidth 8 GHz (24–32 GHz); peak gain 6 dBi at 27.5 GHz; mutual coupling −25/−20 dB at 25/29 GHz; ECC <0.001; diversity gain ~10 dB; footprint 40×40×0.8 mm³
comments: Well-engineered compact array with excellent isolation and diversity metrics; SAR compliance and real-world multi-user interference performance remain important practical considerations.

model_io.model: 4×4 Greek cross-shaped slot MIMO antenna on Rogers RT/Duroid substrate

hypotheses.hypothesis: Circular edges on radiating elements enhance impedance bandwidth
hypotheses.result: Confirmed; measured 8 GHz bandwidth at |S₁₁| ≤ −10 dB

hypotheses.hypothesis: Quadrilateral ground-plane slot optimization improves radiation characteristics and reduces mutual coupling
hypotheses.result: Confirmed; mutual coupling −25/−20 dB and ECC <0.001 achieved

title: Microscopic insight into the origin of super-cooled NCCDW state in 1T-TaS₂ nanocrystals
task: Elucidating the atomic structure and origin of super-cooled nearly-commensurate charge density wave state in 1T-TaS₂ nanocrystals
key_results: Rapid cooling produces mixed-phase configuration with both NCCDW and CCDW structural motifs; suppresses lattice-volume expansion anomaly observed during gradual cooling; SC-NCCDW represents kinetically-frozen intermediate structure
comments: HR-TEM provides direct structural evidence of metastable intermediate states, revealing how kinetic pathways stabilize non-equilibrium phases in layered correlated systems.

hypotheses.hypothesis: Rapid cooling stabilizes a structurally intermediate state between NCCDW and CCDW
hypotheses.result: Confirmed; mixed-phase configuration observed by HR-TEM

hypotheses.hypothesis: SC-NCCDW phase exhibits suppressed lattice-volume expansion compared to equilibrium CCDW transition
hypotheses.result: Confirmed; volume expansion anomaly strongly suppressed under rapid cooling

title: Novel photonic crystal fibre for low-noise coherent supercontinuum generation
task: Developing low-noise coherent supercontinuum source via polarization-maintaining photonic crystal fiber at 1030 nm
key_results: Ultra-broadband supercontinuum spanning 650–1300 nm; relative intensity noise <0.5%; optical phase deviation <15 mrad; phase noise −150 dBc/Hz at 10 kHz offset
comments: Excellent noise performance and wide spectral span make this attractive for metrology and OPA; the unconventional dual-hole design is elegant but manufacturing tolerances require verification.

hypotheses.hypothesis: Input pulse contrast affects supercontinuum generation stability
hypotheses.result: Confirmed through numerical simulation and experiment; RIN and phase noise characterized across pulse conditions

title: Electron and spin dynamics in a single quantum emitter
task: Characterizing Auger–Meitner recombination and spin dynamics in single quantum dots
key_results: Auger–Meitner recombination suppressed above 4 T; electron spin-flip relaxation rate increases above 3 T; spin-flip Raman scattering rate remains nearly constant across 0–8 T
comments: Two-color resonance fluorescence provides comprehensive access to microscopic rates, though physical mechanisms underlying the magnetic-field-dependent suppression warrant deeper theoretical investigation.

hypotheses.hypothesis: Auger–Meitner recombination rate decreases with increasing magnetic field above 4 T
hypotheses.result: Confirmed; suppression observed above 4 T

hypotheses.hypothesis: Electron spin-flip relaxation rate increases monotonically with magnetic field
hypotheses.result: Partially confirmed; strong increase above 3 T but decrease at lower fields
